compiled_state.title: Jira bridge integration tests: free Cloud sandbox + WireMock contract tests
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
## Problem

All bridge tests (outbound, inbound, ACLI client) use mocked AcliClient objects. They verify bridge logic (field mapping, echo prevention, idempotency, dedup) but never make real ACLI/Jira API calls. If Jira changes their API response format (e.g. restructures priority, assignee, or issuetype JSON) or ACLI changes its CLI flags, our tests pass while the real bridge breaks silently.

Closest existing tests: test_bridge_outbound_integration.py and test_bridge_inbound_integration.py — these test the full pipeline end-to-end but still use mocked ACLI clients.

## Current Gaps

1. **No response schema validation** — bridge-inbound.py assumes Jira fields have specific structure (fields.priority is a dict with 'name' key, fields.assignee is a dict with 'displayName', etc.) but never validates this before processing
2. **No ACLI CLI contract tests** — if ACLI changes flag names (e.g. --summary → --title) or output format, nothing detects it
3. **No live round-trip test** — we never verify that a ticket created via outbound bridge actually appears in Jira with the correct field values, or that an inbound pull returns the expected schema
4. **Priority/type mapping is hardcoded** — _JIRA_PRIORITY_TO_LOCAL and type mappings assume fixed Jira configuration; if the Jira project changes its priority scheme or issue type scheme, sync silently breaks

## Research: Available Sandbox Options

We are NOT admins on the production Jira instance, so Premium/Enterprise sandboxes are not available.

| Approach | Real Jira? | Cost | Admin needed? |
|----------|-----------|------|---------------|
| Free Jira Cloud site (atlassian.com/software/free) | Yes | Free | No |
| WireMock/Mockoon mock server | No (contract) | Free | No |
| Atlassian developer instance | Yes | Free | No |
| Premium sandbox | Yes | 53946$ | Yes — not viable |

## Implementation Plan

### Layer 1: WireMock Contract Tests (CI, every push)
- Add WireMock mock server with Jira Cloud OpenAPI spec responses
- Test that bridge-inbound.py correctly parses real Jira response shapes
- Test that bridge-outbound.py builds correct ACLI commands for each field
- Test that ACLI response parsing handles all expected formats
- Runs fast, deterministic, on every push — catches OUR regressions

### Layer 2: Free Jira Cloud Smoke Tests (CI, scheduled)
- Create dso-ci-tests.atlassian.net (free tier, 10 users, full API)
- Generate API token, store as CI secrets
- Scheduled CI job (daily or weekly) that:
  - Creates a test issue with all fields populated
  - Reads it back and validates response schema
  - Updates each field (priority, assignee, description, type, status)
  - Reads back and confirms changes
  - Transitions through status workflow
  - Deletes the test issue (cleanup)
- Catches Jira API changes and ACLI binary incompatibilities

### Layer 3: Response Schema Validation (runtime safeguard)
- Add schema assertions in bridge-inbound.py that validate Jira response shape before processing
- Emit BRIDGE_ALERT on schema mismatch instead of silently producing corrupt data
- Covers fields: priority, assignee, issuetype, status, description, summary
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
